question_text: Use the ray-optics setup shown below. An object is placed in front of a concave mirror at a distance greater than its centre of curvature. The image produced is best described as:

options.A: Between F and C, real and inverted, diminished
options.D: Behind the mirror, virtual and erect

answer_key.explanation: For a concave mirror, an object placed beyond C forms a real, inverted, diminished image between F and C — students must recall this rule; the diagram only fixes the setup.

visual.caption: Object before a concave mirror.
visual.altText: Principal axis with a single upright object placed at a distance of thirty centimetres in front of a concave mirror of focal length ten centimetres.
